params.description: Bags highlight one at a time as skip-count sequence progresses: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16.

steps.text: You're skip-counting by 2s, but you stopped at 5 groups. We have 8 groups here. Keep going: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16.

params.description: All 8 bags pulse to emphasize the count.

steps.text: That's 7 times 2. Count the bags carefully. There are 8 bags, not 7. Skip-count one more time: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, and one more.

params.description: All 8 bags pulse to emphasize the count.

steps.text: That's 9 times 2. We only have 8 bags here. Count the bags: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8. Now skip-count by 2s that many times.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates skip-counting. Bags highlight sequentially: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16. Final answer 16 glows.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags: 8 bags. Each bag has 2 items. I skip-count by 2s, eight times: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16. So 8 times 2 equals 16. That's the product.
